mission.title: valkey: [NEW] Support database level ACL
mission.description: [NEW] Support database level ACL. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current valkey setup
steps.description:
Verify your valkey version and configuration:
```bash
valkey-server --version
```
This feature requires a working valkey installation.

steps.title: Review valkey configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant valkey configuration:
**The problem/use-case that the feature addresses**

Extend ACL to include a way to restrict users to a specific database.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [NEW] Support database level ACL
steps.description:
## cluster: add multi-database support in cluster mode

Add multi-database support in cluster mode to align with standalone mode
and facilitate migration. Previously, cluster mode was restricted to a
single database (DB0). This change allows multiple databases while
preserving the existing slot-based key distribution.

### Key Features:
- Database-Agnostic Hashing. The hashing algorithm is unchanged.
  Identical keys always map to the same slot across all databases,
  ensuring consistent key

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[NEW] Support database level ACL" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## cluster: add multi-database support in cluster mode

Add multi-database support in cluster mode to align with standalone mode
and facilitate migration. Previously, cluster mode was restricted to a
single database (DB0). This change allows multiple databases while
preserving the existing slot-based key distribution.

### Key Features:
- Database-Agnostic Hashing.

prerequisites.description: A working valkey installation or development environment.
